title: Reliability & Trust Infrastructure — Phase 4
consumer_purpose: Eliminate the 20 operational failure boundaries identified in the reliability audit: safe degradation, migration UX, desktop health, secret redaction, plugin trust, event taxonomy, trust tiers, first-run experience, done semantics, and performance budgets
claim_atoms: Trust tiers for agent actions designed
content:
{
  "schema": "rig.relay.phase_plan.v1",
  "plan_revision": 1,
  "phase": {
    "id": "desktop-reliability-phase-4",
    "name": "Reliability & Trust Infrastructure",
    "depends_on": ["PG-001", "CI-004", "CI-005"],
    "admission_condition": "Phase 3 Postgres migration (PG-001) at 80%+; cross-session infra (CI-004/CI-005) operational"
  },
  "context_summary": "Comprehensive reliability audit identified 20 operational failure boundaries. Key gaps: (1) no safe/degraded mode when runtime components break, (2) no Desktop Doctor/health check, (3) event taxonomy not enforced across subsystems, (4) no secrets handling policy, (5) plugin security manifests lack capability scoping, (6) no trust tiers for agent actions, (7) first-run UX is thin. This phase addresses the operational ugliness that separates a good fork from a daily-driver developer tool.",
  "workstreams": [
    {
      "id": "RL-001",
      "title": "Safe Degraded Mode Architecture",
      "priority": 1,
      "effort": "high",
      "description": "Design and prototype the app degradation model: PGlite broken → repair mode. DuckDB broken → analytics disabled, timeline works. MCP broken → MCP panel disabled, core works. Plugin crash → quarantine + app works. Agent loop crash → preserve session, resume offered. Migration failed → backup retained, rollback offered.",
      "design_considerations": "Must be discoverable from crash/error state. Safe Mode panel shows: runtime status per subsystem, repair actions, debug export, log viewer, DB repair, plugin management, config reset.",
      "depends_on": []
    },
    {
      "id": "RL-002",
      "title": "Desktop Doctor / Health Check",
      "priority": 1,
      "effort": "moderate",
      "description": "Build a desktop-doctor command + panel that answers: Does it work offline? After sleep/wake? After restart during tool call? From /Applications? When quarantined? When DuckDB native bindings fail? When renamed? Multiple windows? After auto-update?",
      "checks_included": ["Offline connectivity", "Database connectivity (PGlite + DuckDB)", "App bundle integrity", "Gatekeeper quarantine status", "Native addon loading", "IPC bridge health", "Config file accessibility", "Storage path permissions", "Plugin registry validity", "MCP server connectivity"],
      "depends_on": []
    },
    {
      "id": "RL-003",
      "title": "Event Taxonomy Enforcement",
      "priority": 2,
      "effort": "moderate",
      "description": "Audit all runtime event emission sites against canonical taxonomy. Ensure every subsystem uses registered event names, no ad-hoc event strings. Enforce via schema validation at event persistence boundary.",
      "taxonomy_categories": ["session.*", "agent.*", "tool.*", "permission.*", "file.*", "validation.*", "checkpoint.*", "plugin.*", "migration.*", "system.*"],
      "depends_on": []
    },
    {
      "id": "RL-004",
      "title": "Secrets Handling Policy",
      "priority": 2,
      "effort": "moderate",
      "description": "Design and implement consistent secret policy: detect likely secrets in files, redact from tool output/debug packets/model context, block plugin access unless authorized, avoid storing raw secrets in event payloads. Default event ledger to summaries and references, not raw blobs.",
      "depends_on": []
    },
    {
      "id": "RL-005",
      "title": "Plugin Security Manifest + Trust Model",
      "priority": 3,
      "effort": "moderate",
      "description": "Review current plugin manifest structure, add explicit capability declarations: filesystem read/write scopes, network access, MCP access, tool registration, session event access, secret access, UI panel access, background execution, autostart permission. UI shows capability summary on plugin card.",
      "depends_on": []
    },
    {
      "id": "RL-006",
      "title": "Trust Tiers for Agent Actions",
      "priority": 3,
      "effort": "low",
      "description": "Design tiered trust system: Always allowed, Allowed within scope, Ask first, Require explicit typed confirmation, Disabled in this mode. Map agent action types to tiers. Prevent prompt fatigue by not prompting equally for read vs. delete vs. push.",
      "depends_on": []
    },
    {
      "id": "RL-007",
      "title": "First-Run UX Gaps",
      "priority": 3,
      "effort": "low",
      "description": "Audit current first-run flow against ideal: repo scan → project card (type, pkg manager, test/build commands, branch, dirty status, known instructions, suggested safe first action) → 'Run read-only repo review' offer. Ensure the first five minutes answer: What repo am I in? What can the agent do? How do I stop it? How do I undo?",
      "depends_on": []
    },
    {
      "id": "RL-008",
      "title": "Performance Budgets & Limits",
      "priority": 4,
      "effort": "low",
      "description": "Hard limits for: timeline virtualization, chunked event loading, payload size caps, large-output truncation with artifact references, background analytics workers, query timeouts, file watcher exclusions, generated-file detection, slow-query reporting, per-session storage budgets.",
      "depends_on": []
    },
    {
      "id": "RL-009",
      "title": "CLI Headless Recovery Interface",
      "priority": 4,
      "effort": "low",
      "description": "Boring CLI commands: doctor, status, export debug packet, list sessions, open safe mode, repair DB, tail events, run one prompt, show config path, reset plugin, clear stale claims. Recovery when desktop app is broken.",
      "depends_on": []
    },
    {
      "id": "RL-010",
      "title": "'Done' Semantics Layer",
      "priority": 5,
      "effort": "low",
      "description": "Replace generic 'done' with precise states: Plan done, Patch done, Validation done, Checkpoint done, PR-ready done, Blocked, Aborted, Recovered, Partially complete. UI displays the specific kind, not a blanket status.",
      "depends_on": []
    }
  ],
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Safe Mode opens when PGlite, DuckDB, or IPC bridge fails to initialize",
    "Desktop Doctor reports all subsystem health status with actionable repair steps",
    "Every event emission site uses a registered taxonomy name; ad-hoc strings are rejected",
    "Secrets are consistently redacted from tool output, debug packets, and event payloads",
    "Plugin manifests declare explicit capabilities; UI displays capability summary",
    "Trust tiers are enforced at the permission gating boundary",
    "First-run flow produces a project card with suggested safe first action",
    "Performance budgets are enforced with visible warnings when exceeded",
    "CLI recovery commands work when desktop app is broken",
    "'Done' status is always qualified with the specific completion kind"
  ],
  "deferred_seams": [
    "Agent memory philosophy — deferred pending user trust model design",
    "Multi-window session state UI — deferred until coordination model matures",
    "Operator documentation architecture — deferred to Phase 5",
    "Accessibility audit — deferred to UI stabilization pass"
  ]
}
